Regardless of the situation you are in, pray on it, pray over it and pray through it. God is never late. As you wait patiently for God to move in His perfect timing, praise Him in the hallway.
prayer: Heavenly Father, I come before You knowing that what is impossible for me is never impossible with You. Teach me to trust You to move in ways I cannot see. Help me persevere in prayer and strengthen my faith as I wait on You to open the doors that no one else can open. Amen.

title: 9 June : Show Up When It Matters
body:
Who was there for you during a painful season in your life? How did their presence bless you? Shallow connections are easy to maintain when life goes smoothly. But faithful friends who stick with us through thick and thin are few.

We often ask God to be part of our plans. But do you realise it is a great privilege to be part of what God is doing and to be used by Him to help others in their times of need? Think about the times you ask God to use you, and He sends you to people who are hurting. Offer your practical help and encouragement, show concern, pray for them and lend a listening ear.

Christ does not withdraw from us when our lives get messy or when we fall short. Instead, He draws near to us. May we also become the kind of brother and sister God uses to bring strength to others in times of difficulty.
prayer: Heavenly Father, thank You for always being near to me, especially in my time of need. Thank You for the people whom You have placed in my life in difficult seasons. Give me a compassionate heart to do the same for others too. Amen.

title: 10 June : Joy Is The Cure
body:
Proverbs teach us that "A joyful heart is good medicine, but a crushed spirit dries up the bones". Joy is essential. It is one of the fruits of the Holy Spirit. Imagine possessing all the other fruits, yet lacking joy. Before long, much of what you do will slowly become dry and laborious. Joy gives us strength and delight.

When joy is lacking, it often points to a deeper issue. It may stem from a weakened communion with God, from believing something untrue about Him, from carrying burdens alone or from a life of striving instead of trusting.

Be reminded that God is the giver of joy, and He desires you to live in its fullness. He promises to turn mourning into rejoicing when we yield our hearts to Him. As you surrender afresh to the Holy Spirit, ask Him to fill you with new joy. Our Lord will heal and restore your soul.
prayer: Heavenly Father, You are the giver of true joy. Restore my heart, replace my heaviness with gladness and fill me afresh with the joy of the Holy Spirit. Let Your joy be my strength today. Amen.

title: 11 June : Being An Unoffendable Person
body:
Paul was stoned in Lystra, dragged out of the city and left for dead by the very people he came to serve. God miraculously healed him. Instead of taking offen ce or retreating in bitterness, Paul returned to the city and continued ministering.

Offence takes root when our sense of worth is tied to people's approval or treatment of us. By cultivating a secure identity in Christ, rejection loses its power and criticism loses its sting.

What should we do when someone offends us? Release it to the Lord and choose forgiveness. Holding offence in our spirit weighs us down and harms us greatly. Let it go. Refuse to replay the scene in your mind. Remind your heart that guarding your peace is more important than proving your point. Today, release every offence and receive healing in your soul.
prayer: Lord Jesus, guard my heart from offence. Help me release every hurt to You and anchor my identity in Christ alone. Give me grace to forgive quickly and remain free to love and serve You. Amen.

title: 12 June : Believing The Best In Others
body:
Because John Mark had deserted his first missionary journey, Paul was reluctant to involve him again in the ministry. But Barnabas refused to give up on Mark. He saw beyond his past failure and believed in God's ongoing work in his life.

How do we believe the best in people? We remember our own weaknesses and how grace carried us through our failures. Instead of constantly highlighting someone else's shortcomings, we intentionally look for evidence of God's transforming work in them.

We practice patience. We encourage their growth in Christ. We don't pigeonhole them by their past and their mistakes, or limit what they can become in the Lord. Let us be like Barnabas: sons and daughters of encouragement who see potential when others only see failure.
prayer: Lord Jesus, help me see others through Your eyes of grace. Give me patience not only to believe the best in others but also give thanks to You for them. Amen.

title: 13 June : Calling Fire From Heaven
body:
While many witnessed the dramatic fire falling from heaven, the true power of that moment was Elijah's prayer. His cry was that God would reveal Himself and turn the hearts of the people back to Him.

Today, we may not need a literal fire from heaven. But there are many situations where God's fire is still needed. We can ask for the fire of personal revival to reignite our passion and zeal, renew our devotion and awaken a fresh love for God within us.

We can also ask for God's purifying fire to cleanse our homes, schools and churches. When the Lord's fire falls, falsehood will be destroyed and true worship will be revived. Many will turn to Him in renewed faith. Are you willing to be a vessel to call fire down?
prayer: Heavenly Father, let Your fire fall afresh in my heart. I desire to be on fire for You now and always. Burn away my desire for anything that is not of You. Let nothing hinder my faith in You. Revive me, O God. Amen.

title: 14 June : Christ'S Authority Is Greater
body:
At midnight, Paul and Silas were praying and singing hymns to God. The Lord sent an earthquake to free them from the prison. Though they could flee, they stayed put. Their decision to remain became a powerful witness that brought the jailer and his household to salvation.

This is a wonderful reminder of our Lord's authority and power. Even in the darkest hour, Christ still reigns. What may seem like a place of defeat will become a place of redemption.

Wherever we go, we need to remember that we are under our Lord's authority. If we submit all our ways to Him, He gives us power and makes our path straight. There is power in prayer. There is power in the Name of Jesus. May we learn to walk daily in the God-given authority He has placed upon our lives.
prayer: Lord Jesus, You are the highest authority. I submit my will and my ways before You. I thank You for the power of prayer. Teach me to walk in Your power. I trust in Your name. Amen.

title: 15 June : Taking Time To Tell His Story
body:
In Thessalonica, Paul intentionally took several Sabbaths to explain who Jesus is. He did not rush the message but was patient in answering any questions the people had.

Genuine faith is not built on hype or coercion, but on truth clearly heard and rightly understood. Paul answered questions, addressed doubts and connected the promises of Scripture to the person of Jesus.

Are we willing to slow down and journey with others in their faith? Practically, this may mean listening before speaking, making time to respond to questions about faith, opening Scripture together and walking patiently with people over time. When we take the time to explain the gospel and teach about Jesus with love and clarity, we help others build a strong and lasting faith.
prayer: Lord Jesus, thank You for Paul's example. G ive me patience and wisdom to journey with others in their faith in love. Help me point others to You so that they may build strong, lasting faith in their hearts. Amen.

title: 16 June : Carrying On The Prophetic Mantle
body:
Elijah was taken up to heaven and his prophetic mantle fell upon Elisha. It was a continuation of God's work to ensure His people still had a prophetic leader they could trust. When one servant's assignment is completed, the Lord raises another to carry the work forward.

In our present day, some Christians believe that prophecy has ceased. Scripture tells us otherwise. The apostle Paul urged the believers to desire the gift of prophecy in 1 Corinthians 14. While not everyone is called to the office of a prophet, every believer can ask to be used prophetically in function and in gift.

True prophetic voices have a burden for holiness and justice. They are sensitive to the Holy Spirit's leading. Like Elisha, we can ask for a double portion of God's anointing. Let us answer the call of the prophetic ministry. May our Heavenly Father entrust us with His gifts to build His people!
prayer: Heavenly Father, give me a listening heart and a willing spirit. Anoint me afresh to bless and build Your people according to Your will. Amen.

title: 17 June :
body:
2 Kings 3-4, Acts 18, Psalm 78:56-72

When we read about the miracles Elisha performed, we are often filled with awe. And we tend to assume that such power is reserved for a special few. That is untrue. Jesus told us that signs would accompany those who believe.

The question is whether we can believe that the Lord can work miracles through us. The signs Elisha did were necessary interventions. Oil was multiplied to rescue a poverty-stricken widow. A child was raised from death. Food was purified in famine.

Each miracle met a desperate need. If we desire to see the miraculous today, no pastor or prophet is required. We only need to believe that the same Lord who empowered Elisha can empower us to perform miracles when a real need arises!
prayer: Heavenly Father, help me believe You can work through me. Strengthen my faith to trust Your power in times of need. Use my life to bring hope, healing and help to others, for You are the same God yesterday and today. Amen.

title: 18 June : The Subtle Cost Of Compromise
body:
Has compromise infiltrated your life? Have you made any exceptions that violated God's clear standards? The small concessions here and there will subtly shift our hearts away from full obedience while convincing us that they are harmless. What once felt wrong feels normal now, and what was once resisted is now tolerated. Before we know it, our spiritual life withers.

Leaders must guard against compromise. If a leader tolerates compromise, spiritual drift follows in the place we lead. We must never compromise God's standards for the sake of fitting in. Do whatever it takes to turn from it.

Like Gehazi, how are you tempted to cross the boundaries that God has clearly set? Be quick to confess, renounce and repent of them before they lead to serious consequences for you. It is better to be rejected by people but be accepted by God.
prayer: Heavenly Father, guard my heart from small compromises. Reveal to me the area s of my life where my integrity has been weakened and lead me back to the righteous path. Amen.

title: 19 June : Let His Face Shine Upon Us
body:
The psalmist cries out, "Restore us, O God; make Your face shine upon us, that we may be saved". This was a plea to God for help during a season when Israel was facing military threat, exile and even internal collapse.

The image of th is psalm is that Israel is a vine that the Lord brought out of Egypt, planted and caused to flourish. But now the vine is broken down and ravaged. It is a sobering picture that may mirror the state of our own families or lives.

So how do we experience the Lord's restoring power? We return to Him in repentance and depend fully on Him. When we do, the Lord enters our situation and His light shines into places of brokenness and loss. Let us position our hearts to receive the face of the Lord shining upon us, trusting that where His presence returns, restoration surely follows.
prayer: Lord Jesus, restore my family. Renew my life. I yearn to have Your face shine upon me in glory. I humble myself before You. I depend on You, O Lord. Amen.

title: 20 June : All In For Jesus
body:
"For I am ready not only to be imprisoned but even to die in Jerusalem for the name of the Lord Jesus". For the casual Christian, these words may sound extreme. Yet they reveal the very heart of true discipleship.

Scripture never tells us to desire persecution or hardship. But it does prepare us for the possibility of them. When suffering comes, don't ask why. Ask how we should respond. Will we retreat in fear? Or will we remain faithful to Christ's name?

Like the familiar words of the song, "I have decided to follow Jesus, no turning back, no turning back", let us resolve in our hearts to choose to be all in for Jesus. Hold nothing back, for He alone is worthy of our full devotion and our unwavering obedience.
prayer: Lord Jesus, I choose to follow You with my whole heart. Where You lead me, I will go. My will is not my own, but Yours to obey. Empower me to endure to the end. Give me new strength daily to persevere. Amen.

title: 21 June : Being A Witness Of His Love
body:
Despite being beaten, Paul chose to tell his persecutors how the love of God changed his life. When we have truly experienced God's love, we cannot keep silent about it.

If we say, "God loves us", then our lives must echo that truth. Love naturally overflows. It speaks, it witnesses and it points others to Jesus. It is time to tell everyone we know about the love that has changed our lives.

How can you be witnesses for our Lord Jesus today? We can share our testimony, love people where they are and pray with those who may be struggling. We can invite others to join us at a church service or a connect group. May our everyday conversations be new opportunities to share the goodness of our Lord Jesus Christ!
prayer: Heavenly Father, thank You for changing my life. You have called me to be Your witness and I choose to obey. Give me courage to take steps of faith and to declare Your goodness through my life and my words. Amen.

title: 22 June : Guard Your Heart After Victory
body:
Most of us want to succeed, and we pray for it. But are we truly ready for the wins? It is easy to be humble when we have little. It is much harder to remain humble when we taste success. Pride begins to whisper that we deserve the applause. Success becomes dangerous when we don't guard our hearts. Better to pray to be broken so that the pride of success does not enter our heads and lead our hearts astray.

Success is both a reward and a test. To test whether we will remember the Source of our victory, keep God central, remain humble and not worship the win. Will we attribute our accomplishment to God or to our inflated egos?

Success is good as long as it makes more of Him and less of us. The higher God lifts us up, the lower our hearts must bow. To God be all glory forever!
prayer: Heavenly Father, prepare my heart to handle success rightly. Guard me from pride when victory cones and remind me that every blessing flows from You alone. Amen.

title: 23 June : Choose Him First
body:
Israel was defeated, and its people were led into exile. Scripture is clear about the reason: they chose to cling to their idols. Though they acknowledged the true God, their hearts were divided in their devotion to Him.

Spiritual decline rarely happens overnight. It often begins with small compromises that slowly lead to greater ones. In time, the Lord withdrew His hand of protection, allowing the consequences of their choices to unfold.

This account calls us to examine our own hearts. God desires more than acknowledgement. He desires wholehearted love. May we guard our devotion to the Lord and choose to love Him first.
prayer: Heavenly Father, search my heart. Show me any divided places within me. Guard me from compromise. Help me to love You with my whole heart. Amen.

title: 24 June : Holding Fast To God
body:
Hezekiah was one of the greatest kings of Judah because of his devotion to God. Scripture tells us that he held fast to the Lord and kept the commandments of God. As a result, the Lord was with him and prospered him.

True devotion to God is rare. Out of the forty kings of Israel and Judah, only eight were described as good kings. And Hezekiah was one of them. His life forces us to ask an honest question: What type of Christian are we?

If our desire is to be genuine followers, we must learn to take His Word seriously, put away all idols and obey His commands. Holding fast to God is a daily choice. May we cling to Him wholeheartedly, and may His presence go with us always.
prayer: Lord Jesus, I will hold fast to You. May the words of my mouth and the meditation of my heart be acceptable in Your sight, O Lord, my strength and my Redeemer. Amen.

title: 25 June : Choosing Wise Companions
body:
Scripture warns, "A man of many companions may come to ruin, but there is a friend who sticks closer than a brother". We may have many acquaintances, but only a few true friends. This proverb distinguishes between quantity and quality in our relationships.

Who we choose to walk with truly matters. The people closest to us shape how we think, influence what we value and impact how we live.

What kind of friends do you have and should you seek? Look for those who are walking closely with the Lord. Seek friends with proven character. Choose those who are willing to speak the truth in love. And treasure those who remain steadfast in difficult seasons. May the Lord grant us wisdom and discernment in the friendships we nurture.
prayer: Heavenly Father, grant me wisdom to choose the right friends. Guard my heart from shallow connections and lead me to relationships that draw me closer to You. Amen.

title: 26 June : Faith In A Storm
body:
Though Paul was a prisoner on the boat, he was the one who spoke with authority. Amid violent winds and crashing waves, when seasoned sailors lost hope, Paul stood firm in faith and declared that no life would be lost.

At times, we may find ourselves as the only believers in difficult situations. But faith positions us to become a source of hope and a steady voice of assurance.

When we trust God in the storm, our confidence in Him can strengthen others and point them to the One who still reigns over every wave. Choose to speak faith. Choose to stand firm. As you anchor your heart in the Lord, He will faithfully lead you through the storm.
prayer: Lord Jesus, You are the One who speaks and the storms obey. I have no fear, for I know You are with me. I know that nothing I face is beyond Your control. I place my life fully in Your hands. Amen.

title: 27 June : Rebuilding Broken Community
body:
Under the leadership of King Josiah, Judah experienced one of its greatest seasons of spiritual renewal.

Josiah understood that a broken community could not be mended by superficial changes. True restoration required addressing the sin that had damaged the nation's spiritual foundation. In many ways, the same principle applies to us today. Renewal in our personal walk with God and in our church must begin by honestly confronting what has led to brokenness.

Before relationships and families can be rebuilt, harmful influences must be removed. Restoration and renewal can take place only when idols are destroyed, unhealthy leadership is corrected and false teaching is rejected. Only then can our communities be rebuilt on truth, faithfulness and wholehearted devotion to the Lord.
prayer: Lord Jesus, rebuild what is broken among Your people. Remove everything that does not honour You and restore my relationships and communities by Your grace. Amen.

title: 28 June : The Heart Of A True Servant
body:
After surviving a shipwreck and even a viper's bite, Paul continued to serve as if those traumatic events were nothing.

Not only did Paul have the heart of a servant, but he also truly lived up to the words he wrote, "I am a debtor to this world". He did not wait for life to become easier before serving, nor did hardship harden his heart toward the people around him.

Jesus affirms that servanthood is what makes us great. True servanthood is proven when we continue to seek the benefit of those we serve despite crushing setbacks. It tastes sweeter to live a life of sacrifice to do God's will as His servants than to pursue position, fame or riches. When we would rather relinquish our rights than forsake the gospel. May we have hearts that are willing to seek the joy of others and the glory of our Master.
prayer: Lord Jesus, give me the heart of a servant. You came to serve humanity. Empower me to do likewise. So that the world can see You. Amen.

title: 29 June :
readings_text: NOT EVERY OFFENCE NEEDS A REACTION
body:
1 Chronicles 1, Acts 28:17-31, Proverbs 19:10-12

Sometimes, the wisest response towards an offence is to restrain ourselves. In doing that, we choose not to let offence control our spirit or dictate our actions. Wisdom knows which battles to overlook.

Be careful of the spirit of offen ce too. It may hear things that were never said and perceive an insult where there was none. Reacting emotionally may feel justified in the moment, but it can multiply conflict.

Relationships can flourish when we choose to overlook minor irritations, personality quirks, accidental mistakes or careless comments. These offences are usually rooted in immaturity or thoughtlessness rather than malice. A wise person refuses to give it any weight.
prayer: Lord Jesus, guard my heart and grant me the wisdom to overlook what does not matter. Help me discern which offences to release to You and help me respond with restraint and love. Amen.

title: 30 June : Gospel-Centered Lives
body:
Paul was eager to preach the gospel to the Christian community in Rome. We often assume that the good news is only meant to bring people to Christ. Paul understood that the gospel must continually be preached to even those who already believe.

The gospel is the power of God unto salvation. And salvation is not a one-time event. In Christ, we have been saved, we are being saved and we will be saved. The gospel has to be what binds us together.

As believers, we must never outgrow the gospel. We need to hear it repeatedly, allowing it to renew our hearts and realign our lives. May the gospel remain central to all we believe, live and proclaim.
prayer: Heavenly Father, thank You for Your finished work in my life. May I live each day mindful of Your mercy. Help me be faithful in living a life centred on Your truth and in sharing the Good News. Amen.
